jokes.text: Says who?! The guy who invented custom beer cozies?
jokes.setup: Johnny's confident assertion that custom coasters are effective marketing
jokes.punchline: Moira's sarcastic deflation comparing it to equally silly beer cozies
jokes.explanation: Sharp character comedy - Moira's theatrical disdain for Johnny's business enthusiasm perfectly delivered with specific absurd comparison

jokes.text: Yes, the last thing we want to do is stain these exquisite table tops.
jokes.setup: Johnny's serious pitch about coasters protecting surfaces
jokes.punchline: Moira's dripping sarcasm about the clearly cheap motel furniture
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - using highbrow language to mock lowbrow reality. Character-perfect delivery of environmental irony

jokes.text: tweet us on Facebook!
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to sound tech-savvy with social media marketing
jokes.punchline: Mixing up Twitter and Facebook platforms in classic boomer fashion
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Johnny's well-meaning but clumsy attempts at modernization

jokes.text: Well, aren't you Mr. Futuristic!
jokes.setup: David correcting Johnny's social media confusion
jokes.punchline: Moira's mock-impressed response to basic tech knowledge
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical condescension perfectly calibrated to the family dynamic

jokes.text: If they're at the motel using the coasters, they don't need to know where the motel is.
jokes.setup: David pointing out missing address on coasters
jokes.punchline: Johnny's technically correct but missing-the-point logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Johnny's business confidence meeting practical reality with hilariously flawed logic

jokes.text: A hashtag? Is that two words?
jokes.setup: Discussion of modern marketing tools
jokes.punchline: Johnny's fundamental misunderstanding of basic social media concepts
jokes.explanation: Continues the tech-illiterate Johnny thread with specific authentic confusion

jokes.text: something in a supervisory capacity, perhaps?
jokes.setup: Stevie asking for help with busy check-ins
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately trying to avoid actual work while seeming helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - wanting to help but only in a way that maintains her perceived status and avoids labor

jokes.text: receptionist work is not my strong suit, that's more Alexis' bailiwick.
jokes.setup: Stevie offering front desk work as alternative to supervision
jokes.punchline: Moira using pretentious vocabulary while deflecting to her daughter
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira vocabulary pretension combined with family hierarchy humor

jokes.text: Then I shall keep watch over this front desk.
jokes.setup: Stevie's threat of changing sheets and mattress covers as alternative
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate theatrical acceptance framed as noble duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Moira's dramatic self-preservation instincts with theatrical language

jokes.text: Is it because I was texting in class?
jokes.setup: Teacher asking if Alexis knows why they need to talk
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately admitting to lesser offense while missing the real issue
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection comedy - Alexis's innocent self-incrimination while oblivious to bigger problem

jokes.text: Oh my God, thank you!
jokes.setup: Teacher saying parts sound written by someone else
jokes.punchline: Alexis taking plagiarism accusation as compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Alexis's complete misunderstanding creating perfect innocent comedy through genuine reaction

jokes.text: Trust me, if I could've gotten someone else to write this, I would've.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking if she wrote the paper herself
jokes.punchline: Alexis's honest admission that accidentally proves her innocence
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Alexis's brutal honesty about her laziness actually proves she didn't cheat

jokes.text: Duplicitousness?! I can barely say that.
jokes.setup: Teacher reading back sophisticated vocabulary from the paper
jokes.punchline: Alexis proving she couldn't have written it by struggling with pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Smart character comedy showing Alexis's self-awareness about her limitations

jokes.text: Okay, like, I didn't even choose this font! It's horrible.
jokes.setup: Being caught for plagiarism
jokes.punchline: Alexis focusing on aesthetic objection rather than academic dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis priority confusion - caring more about design than academic integrity

jokes.text: Leased.
jokes.setup: David saying Patrick bought the General Store
jokes.punchline: Immediate, matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Patrick's precision and David's assumption, good character establishment

jokes.text: Give you more time to... oscillate.
jokes.setup: David saying he's oscillating between two business names
jokes.punchline: Patrick's dry repetition highlighting David's pretentious word choice
jokes.explanation: Sharp character introduction for Patrick - dry wit that gently mocks David's vocabulary

jokes.text: Battin' a thousand here, David.
jokes.setup: David unable to provide basic business information on forms
jokes.punchline: Patrick's sarcastic sports metaphor for David's complete failure
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm that establishes Patrick's dry personality while highlighting David's dysfunction

jokes.text: I don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: Patrick's baseball metaphor
jokes.punchline: David's flat admission of not understanding sports references
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - honest confusion about mainstream culture

jokes.text: it's a General Store, but it's also a very specific store.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking for business description
jokes.punchline: David's contradictory explanation that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David character work - overthinking leading to nonsensical contradictions

jokes.text: more like a branded immersive experience.
jokes.setup: David trying to explain his business concept
jokes.punchline: Ultimate pretentious buzzword salad that explains nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of modern business speak - David's pretension creating meaningless jargon

jokes.text: I love the buzzwords, David, but I do need to put something down here.
jokes.setup: David's meaningless business description
jokes.punchline: Patrick's direct call-out of David's pretentious non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Excellent straight-man work from Patrick calling out David's nonsense directly but kindly

jokes.text: I don't play cricket.
jokes.setup: Patrick using baseball metaphor 'change-up'
jokes.punchline: David's wrong sport reference showing complete sports ignorance
jokes.explanation: Callback to David's sports ignorance, showing consistency in his character disconnect from mainstream culture

jokes.text: Oh my God, where did you come from?
jokes.setup: Guest wanting to check in while Moira is distracted
jokes.punchline: Moira's unprofessional shock at doing her basic job
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Moira's hospitality disaster - showing her complete unpreparedness for service work

jokes.text: My associates will deal with all that when you depart.
jokes.setup: Guest asking for credit card for check-in
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious way of saying she doesn't know how to take payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - using grand language to mask complete incompetence at basic tasks

jokes.text: No, I use it to lay out my clothes.
jokes.setup: Moira assuming guest wants cot for company
jokes.punchline: Guest's oddly specific and prissy reason for needing extra furniture
jokes.explanation: Good absurd character detail that sets up the guest as equally pretentious but in different way than Moira

jokes.text: I like to see all my clothes at once.
jokes.setup: Guest's odd clothing arrangement needs
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on his weird organizational obsession
jokes.explanation: Escalates the guest's neurotic behavior, building tension for coming conflict with Moira

jokes.text: Except I haven't decided which bed to sleep in, so that's tricky.
jokes.setup: Moira suggesting he use the other bed for clothes
jokes.punchline: Guest's indecisiveness about basic room usage
jokes.explanation: Continues building the guest's neurotic perfectionism that will clash with Moira's impatience

jokes.text: Yes. - Sorry, which one? - I don't care. - I'm sorry? I don't know!
jokes.setup: Guest asking about pillow types
jokes.punchline: Moira's rapid descent from fake authority to honest ignorance
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Moira's facade cracking under pressure - great pacing in the breakdown

jokes.text: And I'm very much looking forward to the moment when that stops!
jokes.setup: Guest's continued questioning
jokes.punchline: Moira's complete abandonment of customer service politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Moira's hospitality failure - theatrical rudeness delivered with characteristic flair

jokes.text: which is why it was super upsetting when she said it wasn't good. - She what? - Yeah, she said it was bad.
jokes.setup: Alexis describing teacher's criticism
jokes.punchline: Each repetition making the criticism sound worse than it was
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy showing how stories get exaggerated in retelling

jokes.text: duplexity, and backtowards!
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to repeat the sophisticated words from her paper
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'duplicitousness' and 'untoward' showing she really didn't write it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that proves the plagiarism point through Alexis's continued struggle with the vocabulary

jokes.text: did she... happen to say anything about that quote from Lee lacocca?
jokes.setup: Johnny apologizing for interfering with Alexis's paper
jokes.punchline: Still hoping for recognition of his contribution despite the plagiarism disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny character moment - ego and pride overcoming awareness of the damage he's caused

jokes.text: Some guy who's working at Ray's told you your business was a failure?
jokes.setup: David's vague complaint about business meeting
jokes.punchline: Stevie's deadpan repetition highlighting the absurdity of David's interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie straight-man work, using repetition to highlight David's dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: Okay, maybe he didn't call my business a failure, but it was insinuated.
jokes.setup: Stevie questioning David's interpretation
jokes.punchline: David's defensive backtracking while maintaining victimhood
jokes.explanation: Shows David's tendency to catastrophize and then defensively backpedal when challenged.

jokes.text: I'm incapable of faking sincerity. I'm also just incapable of sincerity in general.
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to reassure David about his business
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of her emotional limitations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Stevie character work - perfect self-aware deadpan that's both funny and touching. Near-perfect execution.

jokes.text: I am sitting on a big, empty space, and I couldn't even tell him what I wanted to do with it!
jokes.setup: David's anxiety about his business meeting
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual metaphor while expressing genuine vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good character moment mixing David's obliviousness with genuine insecurity

jokes.text: Hi David, it's Patrick. I um-was just calling to run my business plan by you in a little more detail.
jokes.setup: Patrick leaving voicemail after their awkward meeting
jokes.punchline: Calling David by wrong name while trying to be professional
jokes.explanation: Sets up Patrick's voicemail disaster with realistic nervous mistake

jokes.text: Ciao! I said ciao to that person.
jokes.setup: Patrick trying to end voicemail professionally
jokes.punchline: Immediately second-guessing his pretentious sign-off choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Patrick's immediate self-awareness about his awkward formality

jokes.text: Which that's not - that's not your name.
jokes.setup: Patrick realizing he called David by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Stating the obvious while making the voicemail worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Patrick's voicemail spiral, showing his growing panic

jokes.text: Oh God! Yeah, the text cut us off.
jokes.setup: Patrick's increasingly desperate voicemail attempts
jokes.punchline: Blaming technology for his own rambling disaster
jokes.explanation: Continues Patrick's voicemail meltdown with realistic deflection of blame

jokes.text: I manned that front desk with the vigor of a wartime radio operator.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about Moira's front desk performance
jokes.punchline: Moira's grandiose military metaphor for basic customer service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira dialogue - perfectly absurd metaphor that captures her self-importance and theatrical nature

jokes.text: It was a master class in patience.
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting Moira might have been brisk with guest
jokes.punchline: Moira's complete delusion about her terrible customer service
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony given audience witnessed her complete lack of patience with the guest

jokes.text: with an unrecognizable accent, and scary-looking attire.
jokes.setup: Johnny reading the bad review about intimidating front desk woman
jokes.punchline: Perfect description of Moira's theatrical appearance and speech
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy reference - audience can perfectly imagine how Moira appeared to this guest

jokes.text: And what might this have to do with me?
jokes.setup: Reading review about intimidating woman with unrecognizable accent
jokes.punchline: Moira's complete lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant denial despite the obvious connection

jokes.text: fussy little (Bleep)!
jokes.setup: Moira realizing the review is about her
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately abandoning any pretense of professionalism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Moira's true feelings when her facade is threatened

jokes.text: But unlike him, I will not hide behind the internet!
jokes.setup: Agreeing to apologize to the guest
jokes.punchline: Making face-to-face confrontation sound noble while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Moira making her confrontational approach sound virtuous

jokes.text: Just kidding, I didn't play them for anybody.
jokes.setup: Claiming he shared the voicemails publicly
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal while maintaining deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic fake-out with Patrick's dry humor

jokes.text: I may have played them here on speaker phone.
jokes.setup: Patrick claiming he didn't share David's embarrassing voicemails with friends
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting to something equally embarrassing at work
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation - Patrick's honesty making the embarrassment worse

jokes.text: 'Rose Apothecary,' you know, it's just pretentious enough.
jokes.setup: Complimenting David's business name
jokes.punchline: Framing pretentiousness as the right amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect backhanded compliment that's actually accurate for David's brand

jokes.text: it's just pretentious enough.
jokes.setup: Patrick complimenting David's business name 'Rose Apothecary'
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that's actually perfectly calibrated to David
jokes.explanation: Excellent character dynamics - Patrick's dry wit perfectly matching David's aesthetic without being mean

jokes.text: Would we call that pretentious, or... timeless?
jokes.setup: Patrick calling his business name pretentious
jokes.punchline: David's immediate attempt to reframe the insult as something positive
jokes.explanation: Good David character moment showing his defensive ego protection through semantic games

jokes.text: Why couldn't you have helped me cheat in private school, like everyone else's parents?!
jokes.setup: Johnny apologizing for interfering with her paper
jokes.punchline: Alexis revealing casual acceptance of widespread academic corruption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing Alexis's privileged background while creating touching father-daughter moment

jokes.text: Because I wasn't around to help you cheat back then, and I regret it!
jokes.setup: Alexis asking why he didn't help her cheat before
jokes.punchline: Johnny's honest admission of parenting regret in wrong context
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment mixing Johnny's genuine guilt with completely wrong priorities - touching and funny

jokes.text: is it really plagiarism to take something your dad wrote, and put it in your paper, and claim it as your own? Yes, that's exactly what it is.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to rationalize his help as not really cheating
jokes.punchline: Alexis's flat correction showing she understands better than he does
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal comedy - the daughter being more ethical and clear-thinking than the father

jokes.text: you're better... 'than' that. / Okay, I'm better then that. / Not better then that, better... than that.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to teach grammar while complimenting Alexis
jokes.punchline: Getting stuck in a grammar correction loop
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing Johnny can't help himself with corrections

jokes.text: The drinks out of this machine are very cold. Too cold.
jokes.setup: Guest making complaint about vending machine
jokes.punchline: Complaining about drinks being exactly what they should be
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - guest's continued unreasonable complaints setting up Moira's story

jokes.text: My daughter married a man who turned out to be her uncle.
jokes.setup: Moira deciding to be 'honest' with the guest
jokes.punchline: Opening with impossibly dramatic soap opera backstory
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Moira moment - completely insane fabricated backstory delivered with total conviction. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: The lowlife uncle she believed to have been executed years before by the Indian cartel.
jokes.setup: Moira's soap opera story about uncle-husband
jokes.punchline: Adding increasingly ridiculous criminal elements to the backstory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurd story with perfect soap opera tropes and Moira's commitment to drama

jokes.text: they threatened to burn down the motel, and leave my pregnant baby penniless.
jokes.setup: Moira's continuing soap opera fabrication
jokes.punchline: Adding every possible dramatic element including pregnancy and arson threats
jokes.explanation: Continues the brilliant absurd escalation with perfect melodramatic language

jokes.text: but not without first shaving all her beautiful hair!
jokes.setup: Cartel threatening the family
jokes.punchline: Adding bizarre petty cruelty detail to the epic crime story
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd detail that's both dramatic and oddly specific - shows Moira's theatrical mind at work

jokes.text: Nothing! She is my everything!
jokes.setup: Moira explaining why she works for nothing at the motel
jokes.punchline: Theatrical declaration of family devotion in completely fake context
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to Moira's performance - genuine emotion delivered in completely fabricated context

jokes.text: I would love to read this economics paper.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn praising Alexis's new paper
jokes.punchline: Johnny fishing for credit on work he's not supposed to have touched
jokes.explanation: Johnny still wanting recognition despite promising not to interfere

jokes.text: why shop in store when you can buy online
jokes.setup: Jocelyn describing Alexis's economics paper thesis
jokes.punchline: Thesis that completely undermines her own family's retail business
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor - Alexis inadvertently arguing against David's business without realizing it

jokes.text: Follow us on tweeters!
jokes.setup: Moira reading Johnny's finished coasters
jokes.punchline: Johnny's continued social media confusion now printed on marketing materials
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Johnny's tech illiteracy now permanently enshrined in business materials

jokes.text: We're going to get lots of disciples, dear.
jokes.setup: Johnny's 'tweeters' coaster mistake
jokes.punchline: Moira's unintentionally religious interpretation of followers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira moment - her theatrical vocabulary creating accidentally religious interpretation of social media
